leadText: Quick Feet (Japanese: はやあし Quick Feet) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation VIII 1.2.2 Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] While a Pokémon with this Ability has a status condition , its Speed is increased by 50%. Additionally, a Pokémon with this Ability does not lose Speed due to paralysis . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] If a Pokémon with Quick Feet is in the first place in the party (even if fainted ), the chance of encountering a wild Pokémon is decreased by 50%. It shares this effect with Stench , White Smoke and Infiltrator (since Generation VIII ). Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Sword and Shield only, this Ability instead decreases the chance of hidden encounters by 87.5%. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet this Ability has no effect outside of battle.

sections.title: In battle
sections.text: In battle [ edit | edit source ] While a Pokémon with this Ability has a status condition , its Speed is increased by 50%. Additionally, a Pokémon with this Ability does not lose Speed due to paralysis .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Boosts the Pokémon's Movement Speed when it has any status problems. B S L わるいじょうたいに なると いどうそくどが あがる MD GtI Unavailable S MD When the Pokémon gets a bad status condition, its Travel Speed goes up! MD RT DX When the Pokémon gets a bad status condition, like poison or paralysis, its Travel Speed goes up for a while.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Although the Ability was introduced in Generation IV , no Generation IV Pokémon can have Quick Feet. This Ability originated as a TCG Poké-BODY .
